title: sidalcea spicata subsp. valida
intro: Sidalcea spicata subsp. valida, commonly known as tall checkerbloom, is a flowering plant species within the mallow family (Malvaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Sidalcea spicata subsp. valida can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for topical or internal use.
